methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional community-level AI education infrastructure beyond elite tech hubs, suggesting distributed capability-building through institutional anchors that could spread deep fluency across Richmond's broader population.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capacity-building in a historically underserved region, potentially creating distributed deep skills rather than shallow platform adoption if training emphasizes technical fundamentals over tool usage.

signals.title: Tech briefs: House approves study for low earth orbit satellite broadband in Appalachia
signals.signal_reading: Low-earth orbit satellite broadband study for Appalachia addresses infrastructure prerequisites for distribution, but satellite connectivity alone enables shallow platform consumption rather than deep technical capability unless paired with skills development.

signals.title: Economic development officials: Protect Virginia’s reputation and opportunity for responsible data center investment
signals.signal_reading: Economic development officials from underserved regions advocating for data center investment reveals the gap dynamic—these communities seek infrastructure that concentrates in metro areas while they remain on the periphery, potentially widening rather than closing capability divides.

signals.title: Study: Data center growth adds to ‘perfect storm’ of risk to Potomac River drinking water supply
signals.signal_reading: Data center growth concentration along the Potomac creates environmental strain that may trigger regulatory constraints, potentially reinforcing NoVA's infrastructure advantage while limiting expansion to regions that could benefit from distributed AI capability development.
